title: Streaming Output
subtitle: Streaming Output & Real-time Response Handling
description: AI Agents need to handle streaming responses from LLMs, enabling real-time output, incremental updates, and progressive result display. Streaming significantly reduces perceived latency and improves user experience, but introduces complex challenges in state management, error handling, and data consistency.
sub_problems: Token-level buffering and chunking: How to efficiently receive, buffer, and distribute LLM streaming tokens while avoiding memory overflow and processing delays
Streaming error recovery: Detection, recovery, and user notification mechanisms when errors occur during streaming transmission
Multi-consumer synchronization: Synchronization and backpressure handling when multiple downstream systems (UI, logging, storage) consume streaming data simultaneously
Streaming result aggregation: Aggregating dispersed token streams into structured results (JSON, Markdown), handling format validation and partial result persistence
Streaming timeout and cancellation: Timeout control for long-running streaming transfers and graceful handling of user cancellation operations
best_practices: Use event-driven architecture (EventEmitter/Observable) to decouple streaming data production and consumption, supporting multiple listeners
Implement backpressure mechanisms to prevent fast producers from overwhelming slow consumers, using buffered queues and flow control
Set explicit timeouts and cancellation tokens for streaming responses, enabling users to interrupt long-running operations
Maintain checkpoints during streaming transmission, allowing partial result persistence and resumable transfers
Perform incremental validation on streaming output, detecting format errors early and notifying users promptly rather than waiting for complete responses
